message: Fix RESP integer parsing and reject -0 values (frankenredis-jdi6, frankenredis-zbak)

files.patch:

 {"id":"frankenredis-irk8","title":"EPIC: End-to-end integration test suite proving FrankenRedis works as a real Redis drop-in","description":"# Integration Testing Epic

## Why
Unit tests (1,076) and conformance fixtures (4,055 cases) test internal logic. TCP e2e tests (5) prove basic connectivity. But there is no test that:
- Runs a real workload through frankenredis and verifies all results
- Compares frankenredis output against legacy Redis output for the same workload
- Tests multi-client concurrent access
- Tests persistence round-trip (start → write → stop → restart → verify)
- Tests pub/sub across multiple TCP connections

These integration tests are the proof that FrankenRedis is a "drop-in replacement."

## What Success Looks Like
- A test suite that starts frankenredis, connects via redis-cli or TCP, runs realistic workloads, and verifies all responses
- A differential test that runs the same commands against both frankenredis and legacy redis, comparing outputs
- Tests cover: concurrent clients, pub/sub cross-client, persistence restart, blocking commands with timeout, Lua scripting via TCP","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:20.896335079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:12:31.672075914Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:12:31.671896515Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks complete: persistence restart (e1e9), pub/sub cross-client (v7zc), multi-client concurrency (06pn), WATCH/EXEC transactions (6axy), redis-cli smoke test (42df). 25+ E2E tests now prove FrankenRedis works as a real Redis drop-in.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-06pn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.436452385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-42df","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.828280032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-6axy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:13.963579323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-e1e9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.699590804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-v7zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.569119622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-ix1","title":"Normalize sorted-set zero scores to avoid -0 regressions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-20T20:27:04.284868059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T20:30:12.689735969Z","closed_at":"2026-03-20T20:30:12.687284050Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-iz1j","title":"Implement replication backlog buffer that stores command bytes for partial resync","description":"# Replication Backlog Buffer

## What
The existing `BacklogWindow` in fr-repl tracks replication offsets (replid, start offset, size) but does NOT store actual command bytes. For partial resync (PSYNC CONTINUE) to work, the primary must buffer recent write commands so that a reconnecting replica can receive just the delta instead of a full resync.

## Current State
- `BacklogWindow` struct exists with replid, start_offset, size fields
- `decide_psync()` checks if requested offset falls within the backlog window
- But no actual byte buffer exists — when CONTINUE is decided, there are no bytes to send

## Implementation
1. Add a `backlog_bytes: VecDeque<u8>` field to the replication state (either in ServerState or a new BacklogBuffer struct)
2. Configure max size via `repl-backlog-size` (already a config knob, default 1 MiB)
3. After each write command is propagated, append its RESP-serialized bytes to the backlog
4. When backlog exceeds max size, drain from the front (oldest bytes)
5. Track the byte offset corresponding to the front of the buffer
6. On PSYNC CONTINUE: calculate the byte range the replica needs, send those bytes
7. On PSYNC FULLRESYNC: reset backlog after snapshot send

## Unit Tests
- Test 1: Append 100 commands, verify backlog size stays within limit
- Test 2: Partial resync — append commands, request offset in range, verify correct bytes returned
- Test 3: Offset out of range — verify FULLRESYNC fallback
- Test 4: Backlog wraps — oldest bytes evicted when limit reached
- Test 5: Reset on FULLRESYNC — verify backlog clears

## Depends On
- REPLICAOF TCP client (1v8b) — need connection before backlog matters
- Must be done BEFORE command propagation (1ng7) — propagation writes to this buffer

## Files
- crates/fr-repl/src/lib.rs (backlog buffer struct + methods)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (wire backlog into server state)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:15:36.338674922Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T18:54:13.005179144Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T18:54:13.004893794Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: aof_records Vec<AofRecord> in ServerState stores command bytes, encoded_aof_stream_from_offset() extracts delta for partial resync, PSYNC CONTINUE handler at main.rs:1642-1644 sends backlog bytes to reconnecting replicas.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-iz1j","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-1v8b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:13.676226414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-jdi6","title":"Reject non-canonical RESP integer tokens in parser","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T18:28:28.843361646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T18:28:33.813541504Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T18:28:33.813277123Z","close_reason":"Use strict integer parsing for RESP integer/bulk/array lengths + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-jg50","title":"Add expired/evicted key counters to Store","description":"# Add expired_keys and evicted_keys counters to Store

## What
Add four new pub u64 fields to Store:
- stat_expired_keys: total keys removed by expiration (lazy or active)
- stat_evicted_keys: total keys removed by maxmemory eviction
- stat_expired_stale_perc: percentage of expired keys found during sampling (for active-expire efficiency)
- stat_expire_cycle_cpu_milliseconds: cumulative CPU time spent in active-expire cycles

## Where to increment
- stat_expired_keys: in drop_if_expired() (lazy expiry) and in the active-expire cycle in fr-runtime
- stat_evicted_keys: in the eviction cycle in fr-runtime (handle_eviction)
- The fr-runtime eviction and active-expire code already has the logic — it just doesn't count

## Key implementation points
- Add fields to Store struct (fr-store/src/lib.rs near line 722)
- Initialize to 0 in Store::new()
- For lazy expiry: increment stat_expired_keys inside drop_if_expired() when a key is actually removed
- For active expiry: the runtime's active_expire_cycle already iterates and removes keys — add counting there
- For eviction: the runtime's eviction_cycle already removes keys — add counting there
- Reset in CONFIG RESETSTAT (already wired in runtime)

## Why this matters
- INFO stats reports expired_keys:0 and evicted_keys:0 — essential for monitoring memory pressure
- total_keys_expired and total_keys_evicted are aliases of the same counters
- Redis monitoring tools alert on eviction rate increases

## Testing
- Unit test: set key with TTL, advance time past expiry, verify stat_expired_keys incremented
- Conformance: INFO stats after expiry operations should show non-zero counts

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (Store struct + drop_if_expired)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (active_expire_cycle, eviction_cycle)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:51.722909599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:58.340538240Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:58.340200992Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-jkk","title":"Fix NOMKSTREAM behavior for existing empty streams","description":"## Problem

fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 4000-4007: XADD with NOMKSTREAM incorrectly returns nil for existing empty streams (created via XGROUP CREATE ... MKSTREAM).

The NOMKSTREAM check uses xlen==0 AND xlast_id==None to determine the stream doesn't exist. But an empty stream (created by MKSTREAM) has xlen==0 and may have xlast_id==None, causing NOMKSTREAM to return nil when it should proceed.

## What Redis Does

NOMKSTREAM only prevents creating a NEW stream key. If the key already exists as a stream (even empty), XADD proceeds normally.

## Fix

Check whether the key exists as a stream type, not whether the stream has entries:
  if nomkstream && !store.key_exists_as_type(key, \"stream\") {
      return Ok(RespFrame::BulkString(None));
  }

This requires either a new store method or checking the entry type directly.

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs: XADD NOMKSTREAM check
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: May need a type-checking helper","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:46.076005101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:50.649549505Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:50.647806736Z","close_reason":"Fixed: NOMKSTREAM now uses store.exists() to check key existence, not xlen/xlast_id","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -308,6 +309,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenredis-yys0","title":"Precompute ACL category->command mapping in OnceLock","description":"# Precompute ACL Category→Command Mapping in OnceLock

## What

command_categories() in crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:62 and
commands_in_acl_category() in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:8924 recompute
ACL category→command mappings from scratch on every call. Each call iterates
21 ACL categories × the entire COMMAND_TABLE (~200 commands) and does
split_whitespace().collect::<Vec<&str>>() on each command's flags string —
~4,200 Vec allocations per invocation.

After the April 9 short-circuit (frankenredis-zjii), the DEFAULT user (no
category rules) never hits this code path. But any user with ACL category
rules (e.g., +@read -@dangerous) still pays the full cost on every command.

## Why This Matters

Production Redis deployments commonly use ACL categories for least-privilege
security. A web app might configure: user webuser +@read +@write -@dangerous.
That user has non-empty allowed_categories, so the short-circuit doesn't apply,
and every command pays ~4,200 allocations for category resolution.

## Design

Use OnceLock<HashMap<&'static str, Vec<&'static str>>> to build the mapping
once at startup:

```rust
static ACL_CATEGORY_MAP: OnceLock<HashMap<&'static str, Vec<&'static str>>> = OnceLock::new();

fn commands_in_acl_category_cached(category: &str) -> &'static [&'static str] {
    let map = ACL_CATEGORY_MAP.get_or_init(|| {
        let mut m = HashMap::new();
        for &cat in ACL_CATEGORIES {
            m.insert(cat, commands_in_acl_category_uncached(cat));
        }
        m
    });
    map.get(category).map(|v| v.as_slice()).unwrap_or(&[])
}
```

Also precompute the reverse mapping (command→categories) so
command_categories() is a single HashMap lookup:

```rust
static COMMAND_CATEGORY_MAP: OnceLock<HashMap<&'static str, Vec<&'static str>>> = OnceLock::new();
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. commands_in_acl_category uses OnceLock-cached mapping
2. command_categories uses OnceLock-cached reverse mapping
3. Zero per-command allocations for ACL category resolution
4. All ACL conformance tests pass
5. Users with non-trivial ACL rules see no throughput regression vs default user
6. Memory overhead is bounded (21 categories × 200 commands = ~4200 pointers = ~33KB)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:12.934372724Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T21:39:17.081568068Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T21:39:17.081295221Z","close_reason":"Cached ACL category mappings + command reverse map","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Update the parity ledger and README-level architecture/status notes to match current implementation and evidence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:03:00.145205700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:04:44.629614712Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T21:04:44.629432027Z","close_reason":"Reconciled stale replication parity docs with implemented/tested sync coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-zav","title":"Implement cross-client Pub/Sub message delivery","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:47:33.713475721Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T02:08:03.781137910Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T02:08:03.780200887Z","close_reason":"Implemented cross-client Pub/Sub: global subscription registry in ServerState, PUBLISH routes messages to all subscribed clients via per-client outbox, server event loop delivers via deliver_pubsub_messages sweep, cleanup on disconnect","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-zbak","title":"Reject -0 in Store integer parsing","description":"Store::parse_i64 accepted -0 as 0, but Redis string2ll rejects -0 (INCR on value '-0' errors). Remove -0 special-case and add unit test for incr error parity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T18:34:05.901778169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T18:35:09.649285714Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T18:35:09.648966400Z","close_reason":"Reject -0 in store integer parse + add incr test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-zdw","title":"Wire MIGRATE to Store: complete DUMP/RESTORE key transfer over TCP","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:03:38.670946579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T08:05:23.581427120Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T08:05:23.580671179Z","close_reason":"Already fully implemented by TurquoiseBluff: execute_migrate does real DUMP/RESTORE over TCP with AUTH, SELECT, TTL, REPLACE, COPY semantics","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-zjii","title":"Investigate severe throughput gap vs Redis baselines","description":"# Severe Performance Gap Against Redis Baseline

## What
Investigate and remediate the initial benchmark evidence showing FrankenRedis is drastically slower than legacy Redis across the basic workload set.

## Evidence
From `baselines/` captured on 2026-04-07:

- `SET`: FrankenRedis `1240.93 ops/sec` vs Redis `94402.47 ops/sec` (`1.31%` of Redis throughput)
- `GET`: FrankenRedis `1204.65 ops/sec` vs Redis `91142.35 ops/sec` (`1.32%` of Redis throughput)
- `MIXED`: FrankenRedis `1159.23 ops/sec` vs Redis `96834.08 ops/sec` (`1.20%` of Redis throughput)
- `INCR`: FrankenRedis `1176.59 ops/sec` vs Redis `95183.76 ops/sec` (`1.24%` of Redis throughput)
- `PIPELINE16`: FrankenRedis `1221.37 ops/sec` vs Redis `860900.42 ops/sec` (`0.14%` of Redis throughput)

Latency evidence is equally severe:
- `SET` p99: `58,847us` vs Redis `1,022us`
- `GET` p99: `51,647us` vs Redis `1,038us`
- `PIPELINE16` p50/p99: `693,759us / 857,087us` vs Redis `759us / 1,817us`

## Why This Matters
The `frankenredis-gess` bead explicitly required at least `50%` of legacy Redis throughput for the basic SET/GET baselines. The recorded baselines are far below that threshold, and pipeline depth does not improve throughput at all, which strongly suggests a structural bottleneck in the server/event-loop/runtime path.

## Investigation Targets
1. Profile FrankenRedis under the `set`, `get`, and `pipeline16` workloads.
2. Determine whether the dominant bottleneck is in:
   - `fr-server` event loop / socket IO
   - `fr-runtime::execute_frame` dispatch path
   - RESP parsing / encoding overhead
   - store keyspace representation / DB namespacing
   - per-command bookkeeping (`INFO` stats, slowlog, latency tracker, evidence logging)
3. Produce a benchmark delta report after the first optimization lever.
4. Preserve behavior with conformance evidence before and after each change.

## Exit Criteria
- Root-cause profile captured and checked in under `artifacts/benchmark/` or `artifacts/optimization/`
- First targeted optimization landed with before/after benchmark delta
- Follow-up bead(s) created for remaining bottlenecks if the full gap is not closed in one pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:29:08.439128211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:16.319272656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:16.318401347Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
